timeline.title: Election Timeline
timeline.subtitle: Key milestones in a General Election cycle

announcement.desc: Election Commission announces election dates. Model Code of Conduct kicks in immediately — government cannot announce new schemes or transfers.

nominations.desc: Candidates file their nomination papers. The Returning Officer scrutinises each nomination for eligibility and completeness.

withdrawal.desc: Last date for candidates to withdraw from the race. The final candidate list is published and symbols are allotted.

campaign.desc: Parties campaign actively through rallies, advertising, and canvassing. Model Code of Conduct is strictly enforced throughout.

silence.desc: 48 hours before polling, all campaigning stops. No rallies, no political ads, no social media campaigns during this period.

polling.desc: Voters cast their ballots. Booths open 7 AM to 6 PM. Indelible ink marks your finger. Carry at least one approved photo ID.

counting.desc: Ballots are counted at designated counting centres under observer supervision. Results are declared and winners take oath of office.

simulation.title: Voting Simulation
simulation.instruction: Select your candidate and press the blue button
simulation.successMessage: Your vote has been successfully cast.

forms.caption: Electoral Forms Explained
forms.title: Every Form You Need to Know

form6.title: New Voter Registration
form6.eligibility: Indian citizen, 18+ years old by January 1 of the reference year
docs: Age Proof — Aadhaar, Birth Certificate, or School Certificate
Address Proof — Aadhaar, Utility Bill, or Bank Passbook
steps: Visit voters.eci.gov.in and click 'New Registration'
Select Form 6 and fill in your personal details carefully
Upload your age proof, address proof, and photo
Submit and save your Application Reference Number
A Booth Level Officer may visit for doorstep verification
Your EPIC card will be dispatched by post after approval

form7.title: Deletion of Voter Entry
form7.eligibility: Any citizen can file on behalf of a deceased or relocated voter
docs: Death certificate for deceased voter (municipal or hospital)
Proof of permanent relocation (utility bill, Aadhaar at new address)
Supporting evidence for bogus or duplicate entries
steps: Download Form 7 from voters.eci.gov.in
Fill in the voter's full details and reason for deletion
Attach the required supporting documents
Submit to your local Electoral Registration Officer (ERO) or online
The ERO will investigate and verify the claim
Confirmation sent after the entry is removed from rolls

form8.title: Correction in Voter Details
form8.eligibility: Any registered voter who needs to update their electoral entry
docs: Proof of correct detail — Aadhaar, PAN card, or Birth Certificate
steps: Visit voters.eci.gov.in and log in with your registered mobile
Select Form 8 and choose the field that needs correction
Upload documentary proof of the correct information
Submit and note your Application Reference Number
Verification may include a Booth Level Officer home visit
Updated EPIC card issued within the processing timeframe

form8a.title: Transposition Within Constituency
form8a.eligibility: Voters who have moved to a new address within the same Assembly constituency
docs: New address proof within same constituency — Aadhaar or Utility Bill
steps: Confirm your new address is in the same Assembly constituency
Visit voters.eci.gov.in and select Form 8A
Enter your EPIC number and new address details
Upload your new address proof
Submit — your entry moves to the correct polling station
No new EPIC card issued; same card number at new polling location

epic.title: Verify Your Voter ID Number
epic.invalidFormat: Format: 3 capital letters followed by 7 digits
epic.description: Your EPIC number is printed on your Voter ID card. It has 3 capital letters followed by 7 digits.
epic.caption: Voter ID Tool

problems.caption: Common Issues
problems.title: Got a Problem? We've Got the Fix

name_mismatch.problem: Name on voter card doesn't match Aadhaar
name_mismatch.solution: File Form 8 for name correction. Upload your Aadhaar as proof of the correct name. Processing takes 15–30 days.

not_in_list.problem: My name is missing from the electoral roll
not_in_list.solution: Search at voters.eci.gov.in to confirm. If genuinely missing, file Form 6 immediately. On election day, approach the ERO for a Tender Vote.

wrong_photo.problem: My voter ID has a very old or blurry photo
wrong_photo.solution: File Form 8 for photo correction. Bring a new recent passport-size photograph. The updated card is issued after verification.

double_entry.problem: I'm registered in two different constituencies
double_entry.solution: File Form 7 to delete one entry. By law, you can only be registered in one constituency — your current residential address.

wrong_address.problem: Voter ID shows my old address after moving
wrong_address.solution: If within the same constituency, use Form 8A (transposition — fastest option). If you've moved to a new constituency, file Form 7 + Form 6.

damaged_epic.problem: My Voter ID card is damaged or torn
damaged_epic.solution: Apply for a duplicate EPIC at voters.eci.gov.in. Pay the Rs 25 fee. While waiting, the e-EPIC digital card on the Voter Helpline App is legally valid at booths.

facts: India has the world's largest democracy with over 960 million registered voters as of 2024.
The first general election of independent India (1951–52) took nearly 4 months to complete across 489 constituencies.
Electronic Voting Machines (EVMs) were first used in India in the 1982 Kerala State Assembly by-election.
Indelible ink used on voters' fingers is made from silver nitrate and was introduced in the 1962 general elections.
India's Election Commission was established on January 25, 1950 — now celebrated as National Voters' Day every year.
NOTA (None Of The Above) was introduced in Indian elections following a landmark Supreme Court ruling in October 2013.
The Model Code of Conduct kicks in the moment election dates are announced — it has no legal force but carries immense political weight.
India's Lok Sabha has 543 elected seats; winning an outright majority requires at least 272 seats.
VVPAT (Voter Verified Paper Audit Trail) machines have been mandatory alongside EVMs since the 2019 general elections.
Around 12 lakh polling stations were set up across India for the 2024 Lok Sabha elections — the largest election exercise on Earth.
